evals.prompt: Create a Directus flow that sends a Slack notification whenever a new post is published
evals.expected_output: Complete flow with event trigger, condition, request operation, correct syntax
expectations: Creates flow with event trigger on items.create or items.update for posts
Creates condition operation checking status equals published
Uses nested object syntax for condition filter (not dot notation)
Creates request operation with POST to Slack webhook
Uses array format for request headers [{header, value}]
Uses stringified JSON for request body
Connects operations via resolve and sets flow entry point

evals.prompt: How do I reference data from the trigger in a Directus flow operation?
evals.expected_output: Data chain variables: $trigger, payload, keys, operation_key references
expectations: Lists $trigger variable and sub-paths (payload, keys, collection)
Shows nested object syntax for condition filters
Mentions stringified JSON for request body
